subject: Old - English
note: Practice feed me correct answer nahi hota; jaha ID important-questions se match hua waha answer bhara hai (answer_source).

questions.question_text:
Which is the object pronoun in the given sentence?
They asked him to join us in the lecture.

Which is the demonstrative pronoun in the given sentence?
That is my town, itself is not very large as cities are.

Which is the reciprocal pronoun in the given sentence?
When I asked for help, they looked at each other.

Which is the objective pronoun in the given sentence?
My parents will reward me for taking good marks.

Which is the indefinite pronoun in the given sentence?
None of those, books have belonged to me.

Which is the possessive pronoun in the given sentence?
I respect her concern, but my profession is my first priority.

Which is the demonstrative pronoun in the given sentence?
Those whom I met, can I see them again?

Which is the possessive pronoun in the given sentence?
Your opinion differs from mine in several respects.

Which is the indefinite pronoun in the given sentence?
Most of the candidates could not pass the entry test.

Which is the interrogative pronoun in the sentence?
What are you doing?

Which is the relative pronoun in the sentence?
My mother has books, those really help me in my studies.

Which is the objective pronoun in the given sentence?
They will not oppose us in this matter tooth and nails.

Which is the emphatic pronoun in the given sentence?
She saw herself in the mirror.

Which is the relative pronoun in the given sentence?
We went to the park, whose near to our house.

Which is the interrogative pronoun in the sentence?
Who will be the next option for you?

Which is the indefinite pronoun in the given sentence?
One must careful of his duty, be honest and sincere.

Which is the reflexive pronoun in the given sentence?
We were not able to monitor ourselves.

Which is the reflexive pronoun in the sentence?
The staff members argued amongst themselves for the last hour.

Which is the demonstrative pronoun in the sentence?
These are much better than those items.

Which is the interrogative pronoun in the given sentence?
How can I complete the given task? She was confused.

Which is the indefinite pronoun in the sentence?
Somebody maybe helps you but not me.

Which is the possessive pronoun in the given sentence?
Let her who can do these sums.

Which is the indefinite pronoun in the sentence?
Anything in this world can happen if you believe.

Which is the interrogative pronoun in the sentence?
The car whose color is white has locked.

Which is the singular object pronoun in the sentence?
His lame excuses made him doubtful for me.

Which is the subjective pronoun in the sentence?
She is as intelligent as I.

Choose the correct pronoun:
Show us the art classroom.

Choose the correct pronoun:
Paul, Dave, and ________ are going to play hockey.

Choose the correct pronoun:
It was ________ who baked the English muffins.

Choose the correct pronoun:
________ of the students needed her lock replaced.

Identify the pronoun in the following sentence:
Every year we take a bus trip with the class.

Choose the correct pronoun to complete the following sentence:
Is this big stuffed panda bear ________?

Choose the correct pronoun to complete the following sentence:
You ________ are the only ones who can make a change in the system.

Choose the answer that tells whether the capitalized pronoun in the following sentence is demonstrative, possessive, personal or interrogative:
THAT was the only choice you had.

Choose the answer that tells whether the capitalized pronoun in the following sentence is demonstrative, possessive, personal or interrogative:
WHO finished the last piece of cake?

Choose the correct antecedent for the capitalized pronoun:
Lisa made the invitations and made the decorations. SHE also ordered the food from the deli.

Choose the correct pronoun:
Jenny and Ben introduced ________ to the party members.
A ________ is used in the place of a noun.
Pronouns which listen to something directly from the speaker are known as ________ persons.
There are ________ types of pronouns.
________ pronouns are used to join two sentences.
Pronouns which speak or express about themselves are known as a pronoun of the ________ person.
________ pronoun refer to persons or things one at a time.

Choose the correct pronoun:
Relatives did not come for ________ help.
________ pronoun used to join the sentences.

Choose the correct pronoun:
I do all the work ________.
________ pronoun used to replace persons, animals, and things.

Choose the correct pronoun:
This is the shirt ________ I bought last Sunday.

Choose the correct pronoun:
As ________ cuts it well as he does, I always have my hair cut at Johnson's saloon.

Choose the correct pronoun:
As for ________, I prefer to let people make up ________ minds.

Identify the form of the highlighted pronoun:
Mr. Reeves lost his briefcase at the airport.

Identify the form of the capitalized pronoun:
The last runner was SHE.

Choose the correct pronoun form for the blank:
Dad asked Heather and ________ to paint the garage.

Choose the correct pronoun form for the blank:
The most surprised winners were ________.

Choose the correct word to complete the following sentence:
I wonder if ________ going to rain today or tomorrow.

Choose the correct word to complete the following sentence:
________ house is in Fairbanks, Alaska.

Choose the correct antecedent for the highlighted pronoun:
The class listened as Ted explained his project to Anne.

Choose the correct antecedent for the highlighted pronoun:
A guinea pig must sometimes have its nails clipped.

Choose the answer that tells whether the highlighted pronoun is demonstrative, relative, indefinite, or interrogative:
Which movie did you see this weekend?

Choose the correct pronoun to complete the sentence:
Don't cross the street by ________, children.

Choose the correct pronoun:
Have you ever spoken to the people ________ live next door?

Choose the correct pronoun:
He has got a job in a new firm ________ they work long hours.
_____ two rings here on my little finger belonged to ______ grandmother.
Some of these clothes are _____, and the rest of _____ belong to Kavita.
He claimed to be an expert, but he knew almost _____ about it.
The cat caught _____ tail in the door.
My laptop is not working. Can I use ______?

All of you come to the science lab.
Which is the objective pronoun in the above sentence?

They want to help her.
Which is the object pronoun in the above sentence?

You stole my idea.
Which is the subject pronoun in the sentence?

Those items should be kept carefully.
Which is the demonstrative pronoun in the above sentence?

Neither of those two were passed.
Which is the distributive pronoun in the above sentence?

Everyone was busy in their work.
Which is the indefinite pronoun in the above sentence?

Neither of those books are interesting.
Which is the distributive pronoun in the above sentence?

Which is the possessive pronoun in the below sentence?
All came to ours.

Who are you? Why did you come here?
Which is the interrogative pronoun in the sentence?

No one was at home when I knocked at the door.
Which is the indefinite pronoun in the sentence?

Who was there? I couldn't see it.
Which is the interrogative pronoun in the sentence?

She blamed myself for this situation.
Which is the reflexive pronoun in the sentence?

She came to me for help.
Which is the subject pronoun in the sentence?

John wants to talk to her.
Which is the object pronoun in the sentence?

When I asked a question, they saw each other.
Which is the reciprocal pronoun in the sentence?

We are yours obediently.
Which is the possessive pronoun in the sentence?

I did not see them in the class.
Which is the objective pronoun in the sentence?

How can I prove myself innocent?
Which is the reflexive pronoun in the sentence?

Why did you give them my bike's keys?
Which is the object pronoun in the sentence?

One should help needy people in their surroundings.
Which is the indefinite pronoun in the sentence?

This is the house which belongs to Sara.
Which is the interrogative pronoun in the sentence?

She expresses herself in poetry very imperfectly.
Which is the reflexive pronoun in the sentence?

This is my house.
Which is the demonstrative pronoun in the sentence?

You have your own idea and she has hers.
Which is the possessive pronoun in the sentence?
